chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Time Which, if a Person Reaches it, They Are Considered to Have Performed Hajj

text: 2774 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «مَنْ أَدْرَكَ اَلْمَوْقِفَ بِجَمْعٍ يَوْمَ اَلنَّحْرِ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تَزُولَ اَلشَّمْسُ فَقَدْ أَدْرَكَ اَلْحَجَّ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.2774 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "Whoever reaches the standing at Muzdalifah (Jam‘) on the Day of Sacrifice (Yawm al-Nahr) before the sun passes its zenith has indeed attained Hajj."
